entries.name: Camellia
entries.symbolism: Camellia is an editorial kind of beauty: composed, layered, and quietly confident. It reminds you that care can be steady and unhurried — the kind that looks effortless because it’s consistent.

careBasics.water: Keep evenly moist; let the surface dry slightly
careBasics.temperature: Cool to mild; avoid hot, dry air

entries.appreciation: Look for repeating geometry — petals, spirals, patterns. Write one sentence about what feels “steady” today.

entries.name: Lavender
entries.symbolism: Lavender is calm you can smell. It’s a gentle reset: clearing the mental desk, opening a window, and letting the day feel lighter.

careBasics.water: Water thoroughly, then allow to dry
careBasics.temperature: Likes airflow; avoid constantly damp soil
careBasics.petSafety: Keep out of reach of pets

entries.appreciation: Take three slow breaths. On each exhale, relax your shoulders. Notice one color in your space that feels soothing.

entries.name: Jasmine
entries.symbolism: Jasmine blooms like a soft promise. It’s a reminder that small rituals — light, water, attention — create fragrance over time.

careBasics.light: Bright light, some sun is fine
careBasics.water: Moist but not soggy; don’t let it bone‑dry
careBasics.petSafety: Generally low risk, but supervise pets

entries.appreciation: At sunset (or whenever you pause), name one thing you’re looking forward to — even if it’s tiny.

entries.name: Sunflower
entries.symbolism: Sunflower is honest energy. It faces the light, not because life is easy, but because direction matters.

careBasics.light: Full sun / strongest light you have
careBasics.water: Regular water; don’t let it wilt repeatedly
careBasics.temperature: Warm is good; protect from frost

entries.appreciation: Stand where the light hits your hands. Feel the warmth for ten seconds. Choose one task you’ll do “toward the light.”

entries.name: Peony
entries.symbolism: Peony doesn’t do minimalism — it does fullness. It’s a cue to take up space with kindness: soft edges, strong presence.

careBasics.light: Bright light; some sun helps blooms
careBasics.water: Deep watering; allow a little dry between

entries.appreciation: Pick one thing you’ve been shrinking. Give it a little more room today — time, attention, or a clear boundary.

entries.name: Lotus
entries.symbolism: Lotus is a lesson in contrast: rooted in the mud, reaching for the surface. It’s not about perfection — it’s about returning to your center.

careBasics.petSafety: Generally low risk, but supervise pets

entries.appreciation: Wash your hands slowly, like a reset. As water runs, let one worry go with it.

entries.name: Orchid
entries.symbolism: Orchid rewards precision. Not more effort — better attention. A small adjustment in light, water, or timing can change everything.

careBasics.water: Soak, then drain well; never leave in standing water

entries.appreciation: Do one thing with extra care: wipe a leaf, clear a surface, or make your next cup of tea beautifully.

entries.name: Chrysanthemum
entries.symbolism: Chrysanthemum is seasonal wisdom. It teaches timing: rest when it’s time, bloom when the moment is right, and keep your roots steady.

careBasics.petSafety: Keep away from pets (may be toxic)

entries.appreciation: Make a small plan for the week: one “must”, one “nice”, one “rest”. Keep it realistic.

entries.name: Gardenia
entries.symbolism: Gardenia is tender but determined. It asks for consistency: a stable routine, gentle humidity, and patience with slow progress.

careBasics.light: Bright light; avoid harsh midday sun
careBasics.water: Even moisture; don’t swing between drought and flood

entries.appreciation: Notice a scent today — coffee, soap, rain, or leaves. Let it anchor you for a moment.

entries.name: Hibiscus
entries.symbolism: Hibiscus blooms like a bright yes. It’s a reminder that softness and boldness can live together.

careBasics.water: Regular water; avoid drying out completely
careBasics.petSafety: Generally low risk, but supervise pets

entries.appreciation: Wear or use one bright color today — even a small accent. Let it lift your mood.
